updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.166: Fallback Models, Cross-Session Security Hardening & Multiple Bug Fixes
updates.summary: Claude Code v2.1.166 ships the new `fallbackModel` setting allowing up to three fallback models to be tried in order when the primary model is overloaded or unavailable — a critical reliability improvement for production workflows. The release also hardens cross-session security: messages relayed via SendMessage from other Claude sessions no longer carry user authority, and auto mode blocks relayed permission requests, closing a meaningful attack surface in multi-agent setups. Additional features include glob pattern support for deny rules, thinking token controls, improved error handling with automatic fallback retries, and agent URL filtering in claude agents.
updates.accuracy_notes: Verified directly from the official GitHub release at github.com/anthropics/claude-code, which is the source of truth for docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes/claude-code. All feature details are drawn verbatim from the release notes. No claims to fact-check — this is a primary-source changelog.

instagram.hook: Claude Code just got a major security upgrade — and 7 other features most devs haven't noticed yet
slides: Slide 1: Claude Code v2.1.166 dropped — here's what changed
Slide 2: Fallback models: never get stuck when your model is overloaded again
Slide 3: Cross-session security: relayed messages from other Claude sessions can no longer hijack your permissions
Slide 4: Glob pattern deny rules: one * now blocks every tool — total control
Slide 5: Thinking tokens, retry logic & agent URL filtering — the full breakdown

linkedin.angle: Enterprise AI security and reliability — why this release matters for teams building with Claude Code at scale
linkedin.opening_line: Anthropic just shipped one of Claude Code's most important security updates, and it's buried in a changelog most developers won't read.
linkedin.cta: If you're building multi-agent systems with Claude Code, this release changes your threat model. What security features are you most glad to see? Comment below.

twitter_x.thread_opener: Anthropic just shipped Claude Code v2.1.166 and the security update in the changelog deserves way more attention than it's getting 🧵

youtube_tiktok.hook: This Claude Code update just made multi-agent AI workflows actually safe — here's the one change you need to know about
youtube_tiktok.script_outline: Open: explain the cross-session messaging risk in multi-agent Claude Code setups (10s). Problem: show how relayed messages previously inherited user authority — a real attack surface (15s). Solution: walk through what v2.1.166 changed and how the new security model works (15s). Bonus: demo the fallback model setting — never get the 'overloaded' error again (15s). CTA: update Claude Code now with 'claude update' and check the full changelog (5s).

ebook_blog.title: Claude Code v2.1.166: The Security Hardening Update That Changes Multi-Agent AI Development
ebook_blog.angle: Deep dive into cross-session messaging security, the new fallback model architecture, and what these changes mean for enterprise teams building production-grade Claude Code workflows — with practical examples of configuring the new settings.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.167: Bug Fixes and Reliability Improvements
updates.summary: A minor patch release shipping immediately after v2.1.166, containing bug fixes and reliability improvements with no new feature additions. Likely addresses issues discovered immediately after the larger v2.1.166 release.
updates.accuracy_notes: Verified directly from the official GitHub release. No detailed changelog provided — Anthropic uses this phrasing for minor patch releases.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.165: Bug Fixes and Reliability Improvements
updates.summary: A minor patch release from June 5, 2026, containing bug fixes and reliability improvements. No new feature additions in this build. Part of the rapid iteration cadence leading up to the major v2.1.166 feature release.
updates.accuracy_notes: Verified directly from the official GitHub release. No detailed changelog provided for this patch.

notable_context_not_from_monitored_sourc.title: When AI Builds Itself — Anthropic Institute Report
notable_context_not_from_monitored_sourc.source: anthropic.com (NOT one of the three monitored sources)
notable_context_not_from_monitored_sourc.note: Major Anthropic news published June 4, 2026: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark and Marina Favaro (director of the Anthropic Institute) published a report showing Claude now authors more than 80% of code merged into Anthropic's codebase (up from low single digits before Claude Code launched in February 2025). Engineers are shipping 8x more code per day than in 2024. The report calls for a coordinated global mechanism to slow or temporarily pause frontier AI development if recursive self-improvement accelerates beyond society's capacity to manage it. This story was widely covered by VentureBeat, Scientific American, Yahoo Finance, and others. The Verge likely covered it but is inaccessible. XDA does not appear to have published a dedicated article about this report in the June 4-6 window. Recommend monitoring for delayed XDA coverage.

xda_recent_articles_unconfirmed_dates: Claude Design looks impressive until you actually try to use it (https://www.xda-developers.com/you-dont-need-claude-design-to-make-designs-in-claude-and-it-might-be-better/)
I started using Claude Design and haven't opened Adobe or Figma since (https://www.xda-developers.com/started-using-claude-design-havent-opened-adobe-or-figma/)
Claude Design quietly replaced Canva, Adobe Express, Figma, and NotebookLM for me (https://www.xda-developers.com/claude-design-quietly-replaced-canva-adobe-express-figma-and-notebooklm-for-me/)

I created a complex website using Claude Code, Codex, and Google Antigravity (https://www.xda-developers.com/created-complex-website-using-claude-code-codex-antigravity-only-one-of-them-behaved-like-senior-developer/)
